message:
fix: improve error handling, validation, and config across Go backend, Helm, and frontend (#5333)

Address 20 issues (5311-5330) reported by @aashu2006:

Error handling (Go):
- Log warning on invalid PORT env var instead of silent fallback (#5311)
- Return error from os.UserHomeDir() instead of discarding (#5312)
- Exit with clear message if data directory creation fails (#5313)
- Log actual kubeconfig load/watch errors instead of generic message (#5314, #5330)
- Log StartWatching errors instead of discarding with _ = err (#5314)
- Add writeJSON helper to log encode errors in server_http.go (#5329)

Validation:
- Validate server URL format (scheme + host) in AddCluster (#5315)
- Add default case for unknown authType in TestClusterConnection (#5316)
- Pass specific error messages through in kubeconfig add/preview handlers (#5317, #5318)
- Validate server URL in Connect Cluster wizard before advancing (#5323)

Health check:
- Default to unhealthy (not healthy) on JSON parse failure in MCP bridge (#5319)

Onboarding:
- Show error message to real users when onboarding fails (#5320)
- Accept both monitoring_priority and monitoring_priorities keys (#5321)
- Fix Platform Engineer card generation to avoid duplicate append (#5322)

Helm/k8s:
- Add portable file size fallback (wc -c) for initContainer DB check (#5325)
- Use POD_NAMESPACE env var for CRD persistence namespace (#5326)
- Add rbac.resourceQuotasReadOnly value to scope down permissions (#5327)
- Add startup probe for watchdog to prevent CrashLoopBackOff (#5328)
- Clarify JWT auto-generation behavior in values.yaml (#5324)

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -22,11 +22,12 @@ rules:
       - namespaces
     verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
 
-  # ResourceQuotas - full CRUD for GPU reservation enforcement
+  # ResourceQuotas — full CRUD for GPU reservation enforcement,
+  # or read-only when rbac.resourceQuotasReadOnly is true.
   - apiGroups: [""]
     resources:
       - resourcequotas
-    verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "patch", "delete"]
+    verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"{{- if not .Values.rbac.resourceQuotasReadOnly }}, "create", "update", "patch", "delete"{{- end }}]
 
   # Apps v1 - read-only
   - apiGroups: ["apps"]

@@ -45,8 +45,13 @@ spec:
               # Schema-only DBs are <=200KB; anything larger has real data
               MIN_DATA_SIZE=200000
 
+              # get_file_size: portable file size using stat (GNU/BSD) or wc -c fallback
+              get_file_size() {
+                stat -c%s "$1" 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z "$1" 2>/dev/null || wc -c < "$1" 2>/dev/null | tr -d ' ' || echo "0"
+              }
+
               if [ -f "$DB_PATH" ]; then
-                DB_SIZE=$(stat -c%s "$DB_PATH" 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z "$DB_PATH" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
+                DB_SIZE=$(get_file_size "$DB_PATH")
                 if [ "$DB_SIZE" -gt "$MIN_DATA_SIZE" ]; then
                   echo "Database exists with data (${DB_SIZE} bytes), skipping restore"
                   exit 0
@@ -63,7 +68,7 @@ spec:
                 exit 0
               fi
 
-              BACKUP_SIZE=$(stat -c%s "$LATEST" 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z "$LATEST" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
+              BACKUP_SIZE=$(get_file_size "$LATEST")
               if [ "$BACKUP_SIZE" -le "$MIN_DATA_SIZE" ]; then
                 echo "Latest backup $LATEST is also empty (${BACKUP_SIZE} bytes), skipping"
                 exit 0
@@ -90,6 +95,18 @@ spec:
             - name: http
               containerPort: 8080
               protocol: TCP
+          {{- if .Values.watchdog.enabled }}
+          # Startup probe gives the watchdog reverse proxy time to initialize
+          # before liveness/readiness probes begin. Without this, the pod may
+          # enter CrashLoopBackOff on slow nodes.
+          startupProbe:
+            httpGet:
+              path: /watchdog/health
+              port: http
+            initialDelaySeconds: 5
+            periodSeconds: 5
+            failureThreshold: 12
+          {{- end }}
           livenessProbe:
             httpGet:
               path: {{ if .Values.watchdog.enabled }}/watchdog/health{{ else }}/healthz{{ end }}

@@ -17,9 +17,12 @@ serviceAccount:
 
 # RBAC configuration
 # When deployed in-cluster without the kc-agent, the console uses
-# its ServiceAccount for read-only cluster access + ResourceQuota CRUD
+# its ServiceAccount for read-only cluster access + ResourceQuota CRUD.
+# Set resourceQuotasReadOnly to true to restrict ResourceQuota permissions
+# to read-only (disables GPU reservation enforcement).
 rbac:
   create: true
+  resourceQuotasReadOnly: false
 
 podAnnotations: {}
 podLabels: {}
@@ -112,8 +115,13 @@ ai:
     criticalThreshold: 95 # Percentage at which to restrict AI features
 
 # JWT configuration
-# A random JWT secret is auto-generated if left empty.
-# To provide your own, set jwt.secret or use an existing Kubernetes secret.
+# A random 64-character JWT secret is auto-generated if both jwt.secret and
+# jwt.existingSecret are left empty. To provide your own, either:
+#   1. Set jwt.secret to a string value, or
+#   2. Set jwt.existingSecret to the name of a pre-existing Kubernetes Secret
+#      (the secret MUST exist in the release namespace before install/upgrade).
+# WARNING: If jwt.existingSecret references a non-existent Secret, the pod
+# will fail to start with a CrashLoopBackOff.
 jwt:
   secret: ""
   existingSecret: ""
